truth.papers.A9.title: Microstructures, mechanical properties and deformation of near-rapidly solidified low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-xCr steels
truth.papers.A9.paper_scope: The effects of Cr addition on microstructures, mechanical properties and deformation behavior of near-rapidly solidified low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C steel strips were investigated.

prediction.papers.A9.title: Microstructures, mechanical properties and deformation of near-rapidly solidified low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-xCr steels
prediction.papers.A9.paper_scope: Investigation of the effects of Cr addition on microstructures, mechanical properties and deformation behavior of near-rapidly solidified low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C steel strips, including as-cast, annealed, cold-rolled, and cold-rolled plus annealed conditions.

truth.alloys.alloy_0cr.alloys_notes: Target nominal composition: Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C (wt.%). Measured composition from Table 1.
truth.alloys.alloy_3cr.alloys_notes: Target nominal composition: Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-3Cr (wt.%). Measured composition from Table 1.
truth.alloys.alloy_6cr.alloys_notes: Target nominal composition: Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-6Cr (wt.%). Measured composition from Table 1.

prediction.alloys.alloy_0cr.alloys_notes: As-cast low-density steel strip prepared by near-rapid solidification.

truth.processes.proc_as_cast.description: As-cast strips produced by centrifugal casting under near-rapid solidification in argon atmosphere. Cooling rate ~1–1×10^4 K/s. Strip dimensions: 80 mm × 60 mm × 2.5 mm.
truth.processes.proc_as_cast.processes_notes: Near-rapid solidification refines microstructure, extends solid solubility, and may form metastable phases. This process simulates thin strip continuous casting.
truth.processes.proc_annealed.description: Annealing at 600 °C for 3 h in a tubular vacuum furnace applied to as-cast strips.
truth.processes.proc_annealed.processes_notes: Annealing promotes precipitation and growth of κ-carbides, increases yield strength.
truth.processes.proc_cr20.description: Cold rolling with 20% thickness reduction after stress relief annealing at 400 °C for 1 h. Multipass rolling on a twin roll mill (Φ160).
truth.processes.proc_cr20.processes_notes: Stress relief at 400 °C for 1 h was performed before cold rolling. Cold rolling reduces micropores from solidification shrinkage and avoids drastic structural change.
truth.processes.proc_cr20_annealed.description: Cold rolled with 20% reduction (after stress relief at 400 °C for 1 h) and then further annealed at 600 °C for 3 h.
truth.processes.proc_cr20_annealed.processes_n: This state represents CR20% + annealed condition. Annealing after cold rolling promotes κ-carbide precipitation mainly at dislocation entanglements.

prediction.processes.proc_as_cast.description: Centrifugal casting under near-rapid solidification in argon atmosphere; molten metal poured into a copper mold rotating at 600 rpm to produce strips of 80 mm × 60 mm × 2.5 mm with cooling rate ~1×10^4 K/s.
prediction.processes.proc_as_cast.processes_notes: Applied to 0Cr, 3Cr and 6Cr alloys. A near-net shape method.
prediction.processes.proc_annealed.description: Annealed at 600 °C for 3 h in a tubular vacuum furnace.
prediction.processes.proc_annealed.processes_notes: Applied to as-cast strips of all alloys. Promotes precipitation of κ-carbides without coarsening.
prediction.processes.proc_cr20.description: Stress relief annealing at 400 °C for 1 h, followed by cold rolling with 20% reduction by multipass rolling on a twin roll mill (Φ160).
prediction.processes.proc_cr20.processes_notes: Applied to as-cast strips of all alloys. Eliminates some micropores caused by solidification shrinkage.
prediction.processes.proc_cr20_annealed.description: Cold rolled with 20% reduction, then further annealed at 600 °C for 3 h.
prediction.processes.proc_cr20_annealed.processes_n: Applied to CR20% strips of all alloys.

truth.processing_steps.sample_0Cr_as_cast.proc: Cooling rate approximately 1–1×10^4 K/s. Mold rotating at 600 rpm.
truth.processing_steps.sample_3Cr_as_cast.proc: Cooling rate approximately 1–1×10^4 K/s. Mold rotating at 600 rpm.
truth.processing_steps.sample_6Cr_as_cast.proc: Cooling rate approximately 1–1×10^4 K/s. Mold rotating at 600 rpm.
truth.processing_steps.sample_0Cr_CR20.process: Multipass rolling on a twin roll mill (Φ160).
truth.processing_steps.sample_3Cr_CR20.process: Multipass rolling on a twin roll mill (Φ160).
truth.processing_steps.sample_6Cr_CR20.process: Multipass rolling on a twin roll mill (Φ160).

prediction.processing_steps.sample_0Cr_as_cast.proc: Near-rapid solidification in argon atmosphere; cooling rate ~1×10^4 K/s; strip size 80 mm × 60 mm × 2.5 mm.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_0Cr_CR20.process: Multipass rolling on a twin roll mill (Φ160).
prediction.processing_steps.sample_3Cr_as_cast.proc: Near-rapid solidification in argon atmosphere; cooling rate ~1×10^4 K/s; strip size 80 mm × 60 mm × 2.5 mm.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_3Cr_CR20.process: Multipass rolling on a twin roll mill (Φ160).
prediction.processing_steps.sample_6Cr_as_cast.proc: Near-rapid solidification in argon atmosphere; cooling rate ~1×10^4 K/s; strip size 80 mm × 60 mm × 2.5 mm.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_6Cr_CR20.process: Multipass rolling on a twin roll mill (Φ160).

truth.structures.sample_0Cr_as_cast.overall_st: Austenite-based duplex microstructure: γ-austenite matrix with irregularly shaped δ-ferrite. Nano-scale κ-carbides in γ-austenite, ordered DO3 phase in δ-ferrite.
truth.structures.sample_0Cr_as_cast.microstruc: γ-austenite + δ-ferrite (plus κ and DO3 precipitates).
truth.structures.sample_3Cr_as_cast.overall_st: γ-austenite matrix with increased δ-ferrite volume fraction. Very weak κ-carbide precipitation. DO3 phase in δ-ferrite.
truth.structures.sample_3Cr_as_cast.microstruc: γ-austenite + δ-ferrite (plus minor κ and DO3).
very low volume fraction; superlattice reflections barely visible
truth.structures.sample_6Cr_as_cast.overall_st: γ-austenite matrix with further increased δ-ferrite volume fraction. κ-carbides nearly absent. DO3 phase in δ-ferrite.
truth.structures.sample_6Cr_as_cast.microstruc: barely visible; superlattice reflections almost absent
truth.structures.sample_0Cr_annealed.overall_s: Annealed condition: increased δ-ferrite volume fraction; intense κ-carbide precipitation in γ-austenite; δ-ferrite fully transformed to ordered DO3.
truth.structures.sample_3Cr_annealed.overall_s: Annealed: δ-ferrite volume fraction 14%; κ-carbides with reduced volume fraction (4%) and size (4 nm); DO3 ordered phase present.
truth.structures.sample_6Cr_annealed.overall_s: Annealed: δ-ferrite volume fraction 24%; κ-carbides extremely fine and barely visible; DO3 phase present.
truth.structures.sample_6Cr_annealed.microstru: too small to observe in DF
truth.structures.sample_0Cr_CR20.overall_struc: Cold-rolled with 20% reduction; γ-austenite contains {111} planar slip bands; δ-ferrite morphology unchanged, volume fraction 5%.
truth.structures.sample_0Cr_CR20.microstructur: Slip bands with average spacing 371 ± 47 nm; high dislocation density on {111} planes
truth.structures.sample_3Cr_CR20.overall_struc: Cold-rolled: slip bands with finer spacing in γ-austenite; δ-ferrite 12% volume fraction.
truth.structures.sample_3Cr_CR20.microstructur: Slip bands with average spacing 100 ± 15 nm; high density dislocation structure
truth.structures.sample_6Cr_CR20.overall_struc: Cold-rolled: very fine slip bands in γ-austenite; δ-ferrite 18%.
truth.structures.sample_6Cr_CR20.microstructur: Slip bands with average spacing 45 ± 13 nm; extremely high density of planar slip bands
truth.structures.sample_0Cr_CR20_ann.overall_s: CR20% + annealed: slip bands remain; κ-carbides precipitate mainly at dislocation entanglements; δ-ferrite 5%.
truth.structures.sample_0Cr_CR20_ann.microstru: Slip bands and dislocation entanglements retained
truth.structures.sample_3Cr_CR20_ann.overall_s: CR20% + annealed: slip bands retained; κ-carbides precipitate at dislocations; δ-ferrite 12%.
truth.structures.sample_6Cr_CR20_ann.overall_s: CR20% + annealed: slip bands retained; κ-carbides too small to observe; δ-ferrite 18%.
truth.structures.sample_6Cr_CR20_ann.microstru: too small to observe in DF

prediction.structures.sample_0Cr_as_cast.overall_st: Duplex microstructure consisting of γ-austenite matrix with irregularly shaped δ-ferrite, nano-scale κ-carbides in austenite, and DO3 ordered phase in δ-ferrite.
prediction.structures.sample_3Cr_as_cast.overall_st: Duplex microstructure with γ-austenite matrix, blocky δ-ferrite, reduced κ-carbides in austenite, and DO3 ordered phase in δ-ferrite.
prediction.structures.sample_3Cr_as_cast.microstruc: nano-scale precipitates, significantly decreased compared to 0Cr
prediction.structures.sample_6Cr_as_cast.overall_st: Duplex microstructure with γ-austenite matrix, blocky δ-ferrite, barely visible κ-carbides in austenite, and DO3 ordered phase in δ-ferrite.
prediction.structures.sample_0Cr_annealed.overall_s: Duplex microstructure with γ-austenite matrix, increased δ-ferrite, and increased nano-scale κ-carbides; δ-ferrite transforms to ordered DO3 phase.
prediction.structures.sample_0Cr_annealed.microstru: ordered domains in δ-ferrite, almost all ferrite transforms to DO3
prediction.structures.sample_3Cr_annealed.overall_s: Duplex microstructure with γ-austenite matrix, increased δ-ferrite, and decreased κ-carbides compared to 0Cr annealed; DO3 phase present in δ-ferrite.
prediction.structures.sample_6Cr_annealed.overall_s: Duplex microstructure with γ-austenite matrix, increased δ-ferrite (24%), very few κ-carbides, and DO3 phase in δ-ferrite.
prediction.structures.sample_0Cr_CR20.overall_struc: Duplex microstructure with γ-austenite containing {111} planar slip bands and δ-ferrite; slip band spacing is large.
prediction.structures.sample_3Cr_CR20.overall_struc: Duplex microstructure with γ-austenite containing high-density {111} slip bands and δ-ferrite.
prediction.structures.sample_6Cr_CR20.overall_struc: Duplex microstructure with γ-austenite containing very high-density {111} slip bands and δ-ferrite.
prediction.structures.sample_6Cr_CR20.microstructur: matrix with very high-density slip bands
prediction.structures.sample_0Cr_CR20_ann.overall_s: Duplex microstructure with γ-austenite containing slip bands and κ-carbides precipitated at dislocation entanglements, plus δ-ferrite.
prediction.structures.sample_0Cr_CR20_ann.microstru: matrix with slip bands and dislocation entanglements
prediction.structures.sample_3Cr_CR20_ann.overall_s: Duplex microstructure with γ-austenite containing slip bands and κ-carbides precipitated at dislocation entanglements, plus δ-ferrite.
prediction.structures.sample_3Cr_CR20_ann.microstru: matrix with slip bands and dislocation entanglements
prediction.structures.sample_6Cr_CR20_ann.overall_s: Duplex microstructure with γ-austenite containing slip bands; κ-carbides too small to observe, plus δ-ferrite.

prediction.interfaces.sample_0Cr_annealed.defect_in: Caused by inconsistent deformation of ferrite and austenite during tensile deformation to fracture.
prediction.interfaces.sample_0Cr_annealed.interface: Slip bands extend through grain boundaries leading to small steps; low-angle grain boundary with misorientation of 2.56° observed at high strain.
prediction.interfaces.sample_6Cr_annealed.defect_in: Caused by inconsistent deformation of ferrite and austenite during tensile deformation to fracture.
prediction.interfaces.sample_6Cr_annealed.interface: Slip bands extend through grain boundaries; microbands intersect ferrite.

truth.properties.sample_0Cr_CR20_ann.mechanica: clearly decrease at early stage compared to CR20%

truth.characterization_methods.sample_0Cr_as_c: Uniaxial tensile testing (MTS Criterion 44, GB/T-228.1-2010, strain rate 2.5×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹)
YS decreases and UTS×TE increases with Cr in as-cast condition. After cold rolling, Cr-containing steels show higher strength. Strain hardening rate affected by κ-carbide content.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_0Cr_anne: Annealing at 600°C promotes κ precipitation. 0Cr: κ 7% vol, size 8 nm. 3Cr: κ 4% vol, size 4 nm. 6Cr: κ extremely fine.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_0Cr_CR20: Observation of deformation substructure after cold rolling
{111} planar slip bands in γ-austenite. Spacing: 0Cr 371±47 nm, 3Cr 100±15 nm, 6Cr 45±13 nm. Higher slip band density with Cr addition.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_0Cr_as_c: Measured average densities: 0Cr 6.71 g/cm3, 3Cr 6.69 g/cm3, 6Cr 6.66 g/cm3 using AccuPyc II 1340.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_0Cr_anne: Double-peak between 43.5° and 45.0° changes into single-peak after annealing, indicating almost all ferrite transforms to ordered DO3 phase. Intensity enhanced with increasing Cr content.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_3Cr_as_c: Nano-scale precipitated phase analysis in as-cast 3Cr strip
κ-carbide volume fraction significantly decreased compared to 0Cr. DO3 nanoparticles (size 30 ± 3 nm) densely distributed in δ-ferrite.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_6Cr_as_c: Nano-scale precipitated phase analysis in as-cast 6Cr strip
κ-carbide volume fraction barely visible (very little). DO3 phase (size 28 ± 4 nm) precipitates in δ-ferrite.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_0Cr_CR20: {111} planar slip bands present in austenite. Average slip band spacing: 0Cr 371 ± 47 nm, 3Cr 100 ± 15 nm, 6Cr 45 ± 13 nm.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The product of ultimate tensile strength and total elongation (UTS×TE) is highest in as-cast 6Cr steel strip (38.0 GPa%).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Cr addition increases both volume fraction and size of δ-ferrite, from 3% to 18% in as-cast and from 6% to 24% in annealed condition.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: During tensile deformation of annealed 0Cr and 6Cr strips, dislocation entanglements occur at phase boundaries between ferrite and austenite; slip bands can extend through low-angle grain boundaries (misorientation 2.56°), creating steps.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: A double (Slip-band and Microband) hardening mechanism is proposed: slip-band refinement produces the first hump in strain hardening rate, and microband formation produces the second hump at higher strains. The number of nano-scale κ-carbides controls the early-stage strain hardening rate.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Glide plane softening occurs as sheared κ-carbides reduce their pinning effect, making dislocation glide easier and concentrating the effect of κ-carbides at early strain stages.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The decrease of κ-carbides with Cr addition facilitates the formation of high-density slip bands during cold rolling, causing higher strength and strain hardening rate in Cr-containing cold-rolled strips.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: The measured average density of the steel strips is 6.71 g/cm³ (0Cr), 6.69 g/cm³ (3Cr), and 6.66 g/cm³ (6Cr).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: In CR20%+annealed strips, κ-carbides mainly precipitate at dislocation entanglements; annealing does not eliminate pre-existing slip bands, so yield strength does not significantly increase.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Cr addition expands solid solubility of Al and C in austenite, reducing driving force for κ-carbide formation, consistent with CALPHAD predictions.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Cr addition increases δ-ferrite content and decreases nano-scale κ-carbide content in low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C steel.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The decrease of nano-scale κ-carbide promotes generation of high-density slip bands and increases strain hardening rate.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: A double (Slip-band and Microband) hardening mechanism is proposed for strain hardening of the low-density steel strips.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: The number of nanoscale κ-carbides plays a crucial role in the strain hardening rate at the early strain stage.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: For annealed 0Cr steel strip, the strain hardening rate curve shows a clear double hump: the first hump is related to slip-band refinement, and the second is due to the formation of microbands.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: When the number of κ-carbides is small as in Cr-containing steel strips, the early strain hardening rate caused by slip bands is obviously higher than that caused by microband hardening, presenting a gradually decreasing trend.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: After cold rolling with 20% reduction, the yield strength and ultimate tensile strength of Cr-containing steel strips were significantly higher than that of the Cr-free steel strip; the smaller slip band spacing, the stronger reinforcement effect.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The average spacing of slip bands in CR20% 0Cr, 3Cr and 6Cr steel strips is 371 ± 47 nm, 100 ± 15 nm and 45 ± 13 nm, respectively.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: In the CR20% + annealed steel strips, κ-carbides are mainly precipitated at dislocation entanglements, but the yield strength does not significantly increase because high-density slip bands become the main strengthening factor.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: UTS × TE products for as-cast and annealed strips: 0Cr as-cast 34.0 GPa%, 0Cr annealed 30.5 GPa%, 3Cr as-cast 35.8 GPa%, 3Cr annealed 34.1 GPa%, 6Cr as-cast 38.0 GPa%, 6Cr annealed 32.9 GPa%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: Lattice parameters measured: as-cast 0Cr κ-carbide 0.387 nm and γ-austenite 0.375 nm; as-cast 3Cr κ-carbide 0.387 nm and γ-austenite 0.376 nm; as-cast 6Cr κ-carbide 0.389 nm and γ-austenite 0.384 nm.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_11.value: Annealing treatment increases yield strength of 0Cr, 3Cr and 6Cr steel strips by 131 MPa, 277 MPa and 141 MPa, respectively, compared to as-cast state.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_12.value: The area ratio of (110)δ peak to (220)DO3 peak gradually decreases with Cr content, indicating Cr promotes transformation from δ-ferrite to ordered DO3 phase.
